vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome consists of three primary pots. P1 (Yellow Pot) contains String of Nickels. P3 (Black Pot) contains the Pothos and the White Rabbit anchor. P2/P4 (Black Pot) is present but shows signs of systemic loss of the primary specimen, now reduced to a single, struggling sprout.

inventory_reconciliation.P2/P4: Systemic Loss (Primary specimen absent; only a single sprout remains)

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Stable, showing consistent foliage density.
plant_audit.P2/P4: Systemic Loss: The Mexican Mint/Silver Guest specimens have failed. Only a single, isolated sprout remains in the soil.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: Significant decline. One leaf is chlorotic/necrotic (yellowing/browning). The white rabbit anchor remains in situ.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent dampness observed across all pots; no surface cracking.
biome_observations.debris: White perlite/vermiculite particles present on soil surfaces. No fungal blooms detected.

temporal_deltas.T-4_to_T-1: Gradual chlorosis of the Pothos leaf in P3; the P2/P4 sprout remains static with no signs of active growth.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: The Pothos leaf in P3 has deepened in necrotic coloration (browning at the tip). The P2/P4 sprout shows no change.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: P1 is healthy. P3 is in a state of physiological stress (likely nutrient or moisture imbalance). P2/P4 is effectively a failed culture, with the remaining sprout showing no signs of vigor.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a single, uncatalogued sprout in the P2/P4 pot is the only deviation from the expected registry.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently in a state of decline. While the String of Nickels (P1) remains resilient, the Pothos (P3) is exhibiting clear signs of senescence or environmental stress, evidenced by the browning leaf. The P2/P4 pot has suffered a near-total loss of its intended specimens, leaving only a solitary, non-thriving sprout. The environment appears stable in terms of moisture, but the biological health of the Pothos and the loss of the P2/P4 specimens suggest a need for intervention.
